Each nucleotide consists of a sugar molecule, a phosphate group, and one of four nitrogenous bases: adenine, guanine, cytosine, or thymine.
DNA is found in the nucleus of all cells and is responsible for storing and transmitting genetic information. It is also involved in the synthesis of proteins, which are essential for the structure and function of cells.
